fields.summary: Add golden image configured via Packer and Ansible

content.text: Create golden image using Packer with Ansible playbook. And implement scheduled security scans.
Packer template uses Ansible playbook to install all shared dependencies (git, trivy).
Packer template uses Ansible playbook to save SSH keys of all team members to allow passwordless login.
Packer template uses Ansible playbook to schedule regular security scans and save reports locally under
Vagrant uses golden image for all VMs.
to do golden image configuration, and update

to reduce amount of manual steps to run infrastructure.
Add prerequisites and setup guide to README
Add synced folder to store security scan reports from all VMs
Fixes of small logical and syntax errors

fields.summary: Create additional functions for Flask application

content.text: Create additional functions for our flask application.
Using our flask application users must be able to register, log in with registered credentials and log out.
Logged in users have to be able to create new posts.
Logged in users have to be able to like existing posts.
User credentials must be stored in a database on a separate server.
Post information must be stored in a database.
Post pictures must be stored in AWS S3.
Flask will be used to build a web server.
AWS S3 will be used to store post images.
boto3 will be used to connect to AWS S3.
MySQL server will be used to store information about user credentials, post information and likes information.
Create AWS S3 bucket and configure it so our application can connect to it.
Configure connection from our application to the database.
User information, posts and likes are successfully stored in MySQL database.
Post pictures are stored in S3 bucket.
All users can register and log in.
Logged in users can create posts, like posts and log out.

fields.summary: Create linter for code on GitHub

content.text: Implement linter to check for correct code writing style using GitHub actions.
Objective: Write GitHub workflow that checks if code is written in a sufficient manner.

Specifications: Configure GitHub workflow using YAML that checks if there are changes in any of specified folders to check if code is written correctly according to general code style rules. We use yamllint and flake8 to check for code review.
Configure GitHub to make all the tests pass required.
Task is considered finished when all the files are checked for code writing style when trying to open pull request.
Have created a pull request. Awaiting approval.
hook configuration in the current repository.
Objective: Integrate a pre-commit framework into the current repository to significantly improve code quality by automatically linting and formatting code before it is allowed to be committed.
Specifications: The implementation will utilize official GitHub documentation and
automatically reviews and validates staged code using specific repository linters before a commit is allowed.
To validate the implementation, the developer must:
to ensure existing files pass the checks.
Introduce a deliberate linting error (e.g., trailing whitespace) to confirm that
properly catches and blocks the commit.
